# IIBA BABOK Guide - Cite-Only Pointer

This document is a cite-only pointer. It names the IIBA Business Analysis Body of Knowledge (BABOK) Guide and the chapters the BRD Builder workflow references for stakeholder vocabulary, links to the IIBA publisher, and does not redistribute the guide's text, tables, task descriptions, or knowledge-area summaries.

## What BABOK Is

The BABOK Guide, published by the International Institute of Business Analysis (IIBA), is the canonical industry reference for business-analysis vocabulary, knowledge areas, tasks, and techniques. The current edition is v3, published in 2015. BABOK organizes business-analysis practice into six knowledge areas with associated tasks, inputs, outputs, elements, and techniques, and it defines the role of stakeholders throughout that practice.

The BRD Builder references BABOK by name in two ways:

* As the source of widely accepted stakeholder vocabulary (for example, the distinction between sponsors, end users, regulators, suppliers, and other stakeholder categories) when a reader asks "what is the industry definition of this term?".
* As the upstream authority for the broader business-analysis framework that the BRD Builder workflow informally aligns with. The BRD Builder ships its own original vocabulary (Primary / Secondary / Hidden stakeholder tiers, Mendelow quadrants, RACI variants) and does not adopt BABOK's normative task descriptions or technique catalogs.

## Why Cite-Only

The BABOK Guide is published under a license that does not permit redistribution in an open-source agent collection. The `BABOK` mark is also an IIBA trademark. Embedding BABOK's prose, the knowledge-area tables, the task descriptions, the technique catalog, or any paraphrase derived from BABOK text is therefore out of scope for this repository.

The BRD Builder's posture is:

* Reference BABOK by name and edition when explaining that a term has an upstream industry definition.
* Do not paraphrase BABOK prose. Definitions and rubrics in this repository are written from scratch as original Microsoft content informed by, but not derived from, the BABOK Guide.
* Do not embed BABOK tables, lists, or task descriptions. The stakeholder constructs the BRD Builder ships are documented in [../SKILL.md#pattern-summaries](../SKILL.md#pattern-summaries) as original Microsoft content.
* When a stakeholder asks for the canonical BABOK text, link them to the IIBA publisher below.

## Upstream Source

[https://www.iiba.org/standards-and-resources/babok/](https://www.iiba.org/standards-and-resources/babok/) (accessed 2026-05-25) - IIBA publisher page where the current BABOK edition, license terms, and purchase or member-access options are obtained.
